channel.title: NPR Topics: Education
channel.description: We've been to school. We know how education works. Right? In fact, many aspects of learning — in homes, at schools, at work and elsewhere — are evolving rapidly, along with our understanding of learning. Join us as we explore how learning happens.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Student loan borrowers will get an interest rate cut if they sign up for auto pay
item.description: The Trump administration wants to jumpstart student loan repayment, with federal student loan debt approaching $2 trillion.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration wants to jumpstart student loan repayment, with federal student loan debt approaching $2 trillion.

(Image credit: Daniel de la Hoz)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: As America turns 250, one museum makes history possible to touch
item.description: Federal law requires most museums and other buildings to be accessible to people with disabilities. But access to what's actually inside is often still limited.
item.content:encoded:
Federal law requires most museums and other buildings to be accessible to people with disabilities. But access to what's actually inside is often still limited.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: Big changes coming to student loans on July 1
item.description: Starting on July 1, the federal government will make some big changes to how student loans can be repaid or forgiven.
item.content:encoded: Starting on July 1, the federal government will make some big changes to how student loans can be repaid or forgiven.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Education Department's two biggest responsibilities shifted to other federal agencies
item.description: The Trump administration says it is moving some of the Education Department's most important responsibilities, including oversight of special education and student civil rights, to other agencies.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration says it is moving some of the Education Department's most important responsibilities, including oversight of special education and student civil rights, to other agencies.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Trump further guts Education Dept. by shifting oversight of special ed, civil rights
item.description: The moves to the federal departments of Health and Human Services and Justice, respectively, would further dismantle an agency that President Donald Trump has vowed to close.
item.content:encoded:
The moves to the federal departments of Health and Human Services and Justice, respectively, would further dismantle an agency that President Donald Trump has vowed to close.

(Image credit: Al Drago)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: Socioeconomic factors are becoming 'biologically embedded' in children's brains
item.description: A study of more than 2,300 9- to 10-year-olds found that socioeconomic factors explained most differences in the preteens' brain development.
item.content:encoded:
A study of more than 2,300 9- to 10-year-olds found that socioeconomic factors explained most differences in the preteens' brain development.

(Image credit: Andriy Onufriyenko)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: July 1 brings big student loan changes. Here's what you need to know
item.description: A popular (and generous) repayment plan ends, two new plans begin and many borrowers will see new loan limits.
item.content:encoded: A popular (and generous) repayment plan ends, two new plans begin and many borrowers will see new loan limits.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: After years of declines, young students show gains in reading and math
item.description: Unscathed by pandemic-era school closures, the nation's 9-year-olds showed progress in math and reading. It's a different story for 13-year-olds, however.
item.content:encoded:
Unscathed by pandemic-era school closures, the nation's 9-year-olds showed progress in math and reading. It's a different story for 13-year-olds, however.

(Image credit: Olivier Touron)
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: Sweden set to ban mobile phones in schools
item.description: Long championed as a leader in adopting digital technology, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools beginning in the fall for the next academic year.
item.content:encoded:
Long championed as a leader in adopting digital technology, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools beginning in the fall for the next academic year.

(Image credit: James Brooks)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: In these schools, the World Cup is a teachable moment
item.description: One school district outside Boston is turning the World Cup into a teachable moment, with elementary classes learning about different countries' languages, food and wildlife.
item.content:encoded: One school district outside Boston is turning the World Cup into a teachable moment, with elementary classes learning about different countries' languages, food and wildlife.
item.dc:creator: Suevon Lee
